title: coleus achankoviliensis
intro: Coleus achankoviliensis, also known as the 'Achankovil Coleus', is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant are used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Coleus achankoviliensis include its potential to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its role in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are typically dried and used in the form of teas, infusions, or decoctions, although more research is needed to fully explore its medicinal potential.
